---
title: "Procedure"
---

[← Back to Lab 3 overview](index.qmd)

<!-- TODO (bsb): write a general-purpose field QA/QC note (probably under
     site/autopilot/) covering how to confirm log integrity, signal coverage,
     and parameter capture *at the field* before tearing down. Link to it
     from this page's "At the field — quality check" step once it exists. -->

## Lessons learned

* **Mode Awareness**: It's easy to forget which mode you're in, especially when you're switching back.   You must be in MANUAL mode for navigation % CLAUDE: Check the veracity.
* **Compass Calibration**: Insufficient compass calibration can prevent ARM'ing the USV.  This can also lead to other, more subtle issues.  Try doing a "full" calibration through Mission Planner.  Make sure to secure the USV internals so that you can rotate it in all orientations without damaging anything.
* **QA/QC Data in the Field**:  <!-- note to add separate page on this -->


_Add notes here as they come up — pre-lab, in-field, post-processing. The intent is to keep these tight (one or two sentences each) so they're easy to scan before the next session._

- TBD

## 1. Pre-lab

- Confirm all hardware: boat, batteries (vehicle + autopilot + R/C + ground laptop), 900 MHz radio, R/C transmitter.
- Mission file loaded on the ground laptop. Lab-2 baseline `.param` on hand.
- Roles assigned (Planner / Scribe / Pilot).
- Scribe template ready (UTC time + intent per arm).

## 2. Shore setup

- Standard ArduRover bring-up (see [Lab 2 Procedure §2](../w07_lab_usv_pid/procedure.qmd) for the detailed checklist — same vehicle, same telemetry, same baseline confirmation).
- Upload the mission file to the autopilot via Mission Planner.
- Confirm waypoints render correctly on the Mission Planner map.

## 3. Running the course

- Arm in **MANUAL**, get the boat to open water and to rest.
- Switch to **AUTO** to start the mission. The Planner confirms AUTO on Mission Planner.
- Scribe starts the stopwatch when the USV is *first* within the acceptance radius of waypoint 1.
- Scribe stops the stopwatch when the USV is *first* within the acceptance radius of the final waypoint.
- Pilot switches to **MANUAL** after the mission completes. Disarm to close the log.

## 4. At the field — quality check

Before tearing down, confirm for each run you want to keep:

- The log is closed (autopilot disarmed) and the `.BIN` is on the SD card.
- The scribe captured the UTC arm time and the stopwatch course time.
- Mission Planner shows the boat traversed all waypoints (not a missed mission).

<!-- TODO: replace this section's bullets with a link to the general-purpose
     field QA/QC note once written. -->

## 5. Post-field

- Upload `.BIN` logs to the shared repository, in your team's subfolder.
- Run the analysis script *(TBD)* to generate the trajectory plot and the log-derived course time.
- Assemble the deliverable slide.
